memories.content: ollamas deploys with make ship: typecheck, fresh vitest run, then launchctl kickstart com.ollamas.server
brain embeddings use nomic-embed-text on local ollama; the store is pinned to one embed provider
brain.db runs SQLite WAL mode with busy_timeout 5000 so concurrent readers never hit database is locked
memory tiers rank core above learned above procedural above episodic above working
semantic write-dedup merges near-duplicate memories within distance 0.08 instead of inserting a new row
recall fuses vector KNN and FTS5 BM25 candidate lists with reciprocal rank fusion, damping constant 60
facts are bi-temporal subject predicate object edges; a new assertion supersedes the old one without deleting history
brain maintenance runs daily at 04:00 via launchd plist com.ollamas.brain-maintain
brain tools are hidden from external MCP clients unless BRAIN_MCP_EXPOSE is set to 1
tenant requests are namespace-jailed: brainNs forces ns to tenant id, callers cannot read another tenant's memories
the working tier is a ring buffer capped at 64 rows per namespace; the oldest rows fall off
the drift probe recalls each recent memory by its own content; a low self-hit rate means the embedding space moved
conversation transcripts are distilled every 10 messages into durable memories and facts via a keyless pollinations model
the council endpoint solves a task with three parallel seats, a judge, and a reward ledger
the provider catalog syncs weekly from the cheahjs free-llm list via make provider-sync
Emre is the operator; responses are Turkish, code and commits are English
the zero-new-dependency law: only node builtins plus sqlite-vec; no new npm runtime packages
brain backups snapshot brain.db daily into the backups folder, verify row counts, and keep seven days
git commits are captured into the brain by a pre-commit hook with a three second timeout, best effort
recency decay is per tier: working halves in a day, episodic in a week, learned in ninety days, core never

queries.q: how do I deploy ollamas to production?
which embedding model does the brain use?
why don't concurrent readers crash with database is locked?
what is the ranking order of memory tiers?
what happens when I write a near-duplicate memory?
how are keyword and vector search results combined?
how does the brain handle a fact that changed over time?
when does automatic brain maintenance run?
can external MCP clients see the brain tools?
can one tenant read another tenant's memories?
how many rows can the working scratchpad hold?
how do we detect that the embedding space drifted?
how are long conversations turned into durable memory?
are we allowed to add a new npm dependency?
how long are database backups retained?
do old scratchpad notes decay faster than lessons?
